changes: OpenClaw shipped stable v2026.4.21 at 2026-04-22 04:18 UTC, plus a same-day v2026.4.20 stable release on 2026-04-21 19:19 UTC, which is the strongest direct category-anchor signal in this run.
nanobot shipped v0.1.5.post2 at 2026-04-21 17:38 UTC, adding Windows and Python 3.14 support, Office document reading, and OpenAI-compatible SSE streaming.
Hermes Agent rose to roughly 109.1k stars in the 2026-04-22 scan, extending its breakout momentum without a newer release than v2026.4.16.
PicoClaw published a new nightly build at 2026-04-22 00:59 UTC, while the rest of the watched set mostly showed routine growth and fresh pushes.
whyItMatters: OpenClaw itself moved, so this is not just derivative churn. The anchor project is still shipping fast and setting the pace.
nanobot continues to validate the lightweight lane as a serious product branch, not just a one-off clone story.
Hermes keeps compounding adjacent-market momentum, but today's movement still strengthens the existing strategic read rather than changing the map.
